title: coreopsis cordifolia
intro: Coreopsis cordifolia, also known as Heartleaf Tickseed, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and small, yellow or orange flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The root and leaves of Coreopsis cordifolia have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Coreopsis cordifolia can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies.
